So we can include "priority_threshold":112 maybe. Also "depth": maybe 1? Not sure. We can just include "target_selector":"F1","priority_threshold":112. Might also include "limit":28? Actually limit is optional. Could set "limit":28. Use "relation":"-" maybe not needed. Let's include "relation":"-"? Actually relation filters maybe a field "relation_filters"? Not sure. The contract says "relation filters". Could be a field "relation_filters". But we can keep minimal.

Thus tool_call: {"tool_name":"session.expand_file","payload":{"target_selector":"F1","priority_threshold":112,"limit":28}}.

We need to include acquired_fact_ids: maybe the result will yield some fact ids, but we don't have them yet. Since we haven't executed, we cannot include acquired_fact_ids. The shape requires acquired_fact_ids list. It can be empty array if none acquired yet. So we can set "acquired_fact_ids":[].

Readiness_state: after a tool_call, we are not ready to edit yet; we have performed a tool call, so readiness_state maybe "evidence_acquired"? Actually after tool call, we may be "evidence_acquired"? The readiness_state options: "not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit". Since we just made a tool call to gather evidence, we likely are "evidence_acquired"? But we haven't acquired yet; after call returns we will have evidence. But currently we are planning to call. So maybe "evidence_visible"? Hmm.

The schema: When kind is tool_call, readiness_state can be any? It says "readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit". Since we are about to call tool, we have not yet acquired evidence, but we are actively seeking. Probably "evidence_visible"? Not sure. I'd set "readiness_state":"not_ready". Because we are not ready to edit yet. But maybe "evidence_visible" indicates we have some visible evidence but not yet ready. I'll choose "evidence_visible". Actually we have some evidence visible from earlier steps (they already expanded etc). But for this step, we are about to expand. I'd set "readiness_state":"evidence_acquired"? Wait that indicates we have acquired evidence and ready to edit? No, ready_to_edit is separate. So "evidence_acquired" means we have evidence but not ready to edit? Actually readiness states: not_ready (nothing), evidence_visible (some evidence visible), evidence_acquired (evidence acquired), ready_to_edit (ready). So after we call expand_file, we will acquire new evidence; but currently we haven't. So maybe "evidence_visible". I'll use "evidence_visible". That indicates there is some visible evidence (maybe from earlier). So readiness_state: "evidence_visible".
